global_rules.hv: HV (Handlingsvärde) = action value. Bonuses expressed as +1 HV, +2 HV etc. on specific actions or sub-stats.
global_rules.cv: CV = casting value used in crystal magic.
global_rules.kv: KV = ritual power value used in shaman rituals.
global_rules.grid_weight: Weight always expressed as AxB grid squares (e.g. 1x1, 2x2, 3x2).
global_rules.magical_cost: Magical items with powerful effects require a cost that is specific and tied to the item's identity. Choose one: bodily toll | usage condition | social/legal risk | limited frequency | ritual dependency | binding to a power | public marking | moral cost.
global_rules.misc_rule: Use MISC when an item genuinely does not fit a rule-backed category. It is a last resort, not a default.
global_rules.effect_rule: Effects must be self-explanatory. Trigger and outcome must be explicit. Forbidden verbs: påverkar, vägleder, resonerar, kan avslöja, fungerar konstigt, influences, guides, resonates.
global_rules.cost_rule: Do not generate magical costs. Only generate limitation (when/how the item cannot be used) or drawback (negative consequence directly tied to the function) when they are inseparable from the item's identity.
global_rules.naming_rule: Item names must be Swedish compounds built from function, behavior, consequence, or material truth. Short and direct. No decorative or atmospheric names that only describe mood.

damage_scale.note: A span weapon rolls with greater variance — minimum damage is the lower tier, maximum approaches the higher tier. EXTREME alone or in a span is rare and reserved for the most powerful weapons.

MELEE.profile_format: A/An [adjective] [material+weapon type] that deals [damage] and weighs [AxB]. Also [effect]. However [drawback if warranted].
requirements: Damage level or span from damage_scale

categories.Hackvapen: Bladed or edged. Axes, toothed blades, obsidian knives, stone-age glaives. Cuts and cleaves.
categories.Trubbvapen: Blunt impact. Clubs, staves, bone clubs. Crushes and stuns.
categories.Repvapen: Flexible component. Whips, flails, sinew-linked weapons. Difficult to block.
categories.Stickvapen: Piercing point. Spears, bone daggers, jabbing implements. Reach and precision.

requirements: Damage level or span from damage_scale
Specify if ammunition required — if yes, weapon is unusable without it

categories.Kastvapen: Thrown axes, spears, knives. Often usable in melee as well.
categories.Pilbågar: Requires arrows. High range and precision.
categories.Slungor: Requires projectiles (stone, bone). Simple but powerful.
categories.Blåsvapen: Requires projectiles (darts, thorns). Silent, often poisoned.

requirements: Consumption rule — number of uses or single-use effect
guidelines: Modifies weapon damage or function situationally
Cannot permanently alter weapon base profile

Medium.notes: Difficult to combine with two-handed weapons.

SHIELD.shield_bash: On a successful block of +2 HV or more — push target back, brief stun (lose next reaction).

KRISTALLSEJDARE.description: Focus items for crystal mages. Almost always incorporate colored crystals in some form — set into the object, inscribed, or carried loose. Color corresponds to lore.
effect_directions: CV bonuses on spellcasting
Spell dice — extra dice from the mana pool
Mana pool — store dice for future use, borrow from upcoming pool
Miscast — dampen, delay, or redirect miscast effects
Strengthen a specific lore's expression, range, or targets
Temporarily swap or borrow spells from another lore
Bind a specific spell to the item — faster or cheaper to cast
Store a successfully cast spell for later use
KRISTALLSEJDARE.note: Magical items carry a cost tied to the lore's emotional character — a wrath-lore item might cost calm, a sorrow-lore item might demand a memory.
KRISTALLSEJDARE.miscast_rule: Miscast: when two sixes are rolled during a spellcasting check, roll on the miscast table.
KRISTALLSEJDARE.religion_rule: Crystal magic (Kristallsejdare) is emotional and elemental, never religious. No prayers, offerings, or divine invocations belong in crystal magic.

SHAMAN.description: Ritual tools for shamans. Natural and organic — bone, roots, dried animal parts, stone, bark, fungus. Worn and personal, bearing the marks of many rituals.
effect_directions: KV modifications situationally
Stabilize a ritual on KV shortfall — reduce risk of backlash
Strengthen a specific ritual type (healing, divination, binding, weather, blood ritual)
Bind a ritual to a place so it can be repeated
Help find, attract, or identify specific ingredients
Count as an ingredient type without being consumed
Extend a ritual's effect to additional targets
Remember a successful ritual and make it easier to repeat
SHAMAN.note: Magical items carry a concrete natural cost — must be buried after use, requires blood, functions only in specific terrain.

SHAMAN_INGREDIENT.description: Consumable ingredient cards for shaman rituals. Each ingredient contributes its die value to KV when used in a ritual.

structure.ingredient_type: Fungi | Animal parts | Essence | Minerals | Plants

ingredient_types.Essence: Elemental rituals, natural attunement, sensing the world

SHAMAN_INGREDIENT.note: Rare and Mythic ingredients may carry an additional situational effect beyond their KV contribution.

LYÅDSKAPARE.description: Items for sound mages. Focused on sound, trance, and music magic — not limited to instruments. Includes anything that interacts with how sound moves, how trance is entered, or how music affects the world.
effect_directions: Instruments — protect, strengthen, store melodies
Trance — shorten, stabilize, protect during vulnerable phase
Range and targets for music spells
Allow sound to travel through unusual media — stone, water, bone
Strengthen music's morale effect on listeners
Store a specific melody or tone for later release
Items that already vibrate at the right frequency — ease entry into trance
Disrupt or block another sound mage's magic
LYÅDSKAPARE.note: Magical items carry a cost tied to sound or instrument — the item may not be able to fall silent, or it binds the bearer to a specific direction of magic.

ORAKEL.description: Relics and sacred objects for oracles. Old and carried — inscribed stones, fossils, sacred bones, offering vessels. What matters is that they have belonged to something — a place, an event, a previous negotiation.
effect_directions: Improve negotiation position before an emissary
Reduce offering requirement or make a simpler offering more meaningful
Soften consequences of a poor negotiation outcome
Store a blessing for a specific future moment
Make signs and omens clearer — but not easier to interpret
Strengthen a specific god's or aspect's inclination to respond
Protect against divine wrath without eliminating it
ORAKEL.note: Magical items carry a cost tied to the divine — must be returned to a sacred place after use, or loses power if the oracle breaks their god's code.

ADVENIRE.description: Items shaped by contact with Advenire through one of the four lore traditions. Requires no lore knowledge to use — the effect belongs to the item itself, not the bearer.
ADVENIRE.lore_origin_field: Specify lore_origin: KRISTALLSEJDARE | SHAMAN | LYÅDSKAPARE | ORAKEL

KRISTALLSEJDARE.description: Item imbued with one of the six lores and its feeling, color, and element.

lores.Red / Wrath: Fire and smoke. Explosive, destructive, aggressively controlled.
lores.Green / Shame: Plants and animals. Ensnaring, judging force of nature.
lores.Yellow / Joy: Light and healing. Warm, hopeful, protective.
lores.Purple / Pride: Lightning and necromancy. Arrogant, dominant, hard.
lores.Orange / Love: Stone and sand. Heavy, stable, sheltering.
lores.Blue / Sorrow: Water and wind. Melancholic, still, suddenly storming.

SHAMAN.description: Item blessed in rituals or with shamanic ingredients incorporated into it. Nature's force channeled into an object — gives unique effects rooted in the ingredient's nature and the ritual's intention.

LYÅDSKAPARE.description: A sound mage's soul or tone locked into an object. The item carries the nature of music magic permanently and gives unexpected properties tied to sound, vibration, or movement.

ORAKEL.description: Item tied to divine emissaries or powerful religious events. Carries a god's or aspect's nature and gives unexpected effects related to that god's domain.

PETS.description: All animals are hybrids of two real animals. Specify size and 1–2 evolutions.

sizes.Pocket: Mouse-sized. Fits in pocket or bag.
sizes.Foot: Cat or dog-sized. Follows on foot.

PETS.evolutions: Innate abilities tied to the animal's nature. Max 2 at purchase. More can be bought with experience points.
PETS.note: Pets do not replace player character core roles.

MISC.description: Everything that does not fit a rule-backed category. Jewelry, clothing, tools, contracts, maps, organic objects, relics, carrying equipment, instruments without lore connection, and anything else creative.
MISC.type_field_instruction: Describe what the item is in the type field — 'contract', 'amulet', 'cloak', 'map', 'herb bundle', 'carved idol', etc.
MISC.note: No mechanical restrictions beyond global rules. Use MISC when nothing else fits — not as a default.
